blockingIssues.description: Oversized request handling can emit concatenated JSON bodies because body-limit response interception writes an API error and the handler path can still write another error payload (internal/shared/httputil/bodylimit.go).
Password change handler logs session-revocation failure but still returns 200, so revocation is not guaranteed immediate; required revocation behavior tests were also not implemented.
Default runtime HTTPS write timeout remains ColdStartTimeout+15s (2m15s under default config), not the required fixed 30s (VAL-HEADERS-004).
Pending-member approve/reject endpoints update membership status by org+user without constraining current status to pending, allowing active memberships to be altered unexpectedly.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated .factory/library/architecture.md to reflect current edge proxy behavior: inbound forwarding headers are stripped and replaced in Rewrite handlers.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update security-backend-worker guidance to explicitly cover ReverseProxy Director vs Rewrite forwarding-header behavior so workers avoid duplicated/unsanitized XFF propagation.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: xff-sanitization review observed this behavior as central to correctness and currently under-documented in skill guidance.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require validation of config-driven defaults via real startup/default wiring (not only unit assertions with hand-picked config values) when contracts specify runtime defaults.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: security-response-headers review passed tests while default runtime timeout remained non-compliant with VAL-HEADERS-004.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify enforcement around RED-first workflow and truthful skillFeedback reporting; add escalation guidance when required DB-backed assertions cannot be executed.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Multiple reviews reported workers marking procedure followed despite implementation-first flow or reduced coverage due to missing DB test strategy.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Resolve contradictory schema-change guidance for security-hardening missions by making OAuth-related schema allowances explicit and unambiguous.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: oauth-auto-creation-controls review found mission AGENTS says schema is off-limits while a later section explicitly allows schema changes for OAuth domain matching.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Align security-backend-worker schema-change policy with mission-specific exceptions so workers do not receive conflicting instructions.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: oauth-auto-creation-controls review found SKILL.md directs returning to orchestrator for schema work, conflicting with mission requirements that required schema additions.

rejectedObservations.observation: Create a new shared DB-backed handler test harness note immediately for internal/rpc.
Add a generic library entry for WriteAPIError/body-limit double-write now.
